subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
- **1 Pond Board Tile** (starting tile)
- **28 Plot Tiles** (11 green, 9 yellow, 7 pink, 1 gardener starting tile)
- **36 Bamboo Sections** (12 of each color: green, yellow, pink)
- **20 Irrigation Channels**
- **9 Improvements** (3 of each: watershed, fertilizer, enclosure)
- **46 Objective Cards** (15 plot objectives, 16 panda objectives, 15 gardener objectives)
- **1 Weather Die**
- **4 Individual Boards**
- **8 Action Tokens**
- **1 Gardener Figure**
- **1 Panda Figure**
- **1 Emperor Card** (awards 2 bonus points to player who triggers endgame)

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place Pond Tile** — Put the pond board tile in the center of the table.
2. **Place Gardener & Panda** — Both figures start on the pond tile.
3. **Shuffle Plot Tiles** — Create 3 face-down stacks of plot tiles (one for each color).
4. **Prepare Irrigation** — Place all 20 irrigation channels in a supply near the pond.
5. **Prepare Bamboo Supply** — Keep bamboo sections organized by color (green, yellow, pink) in the supply.
6. **Prepare Improvements** — Keep the 9 improvement chips in a supply.
7. **Shuffle Objectives** — Create 3 separate decks (plot, panda, gardener objectives).
8. **Place Weather Die** — Keep it accessible for all players.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
Each player receives:
- **1 Individual Board** (for tracking irrigations and improvements)
- **2 Action Tokens** (for taking actions)
- **1 Objective Card** of each type (draw 1 plot, 1 panda, 1 gardener objective)

Objective cards are kept secret. Players can hold up to 5 objective cards at a time.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
**First Player** — The player who most recently ate bamboo shoots goes first (or choose randomly).

**Turn Order** — Play proceeds clockwise.

**Starting Garden** — The game begins with only the pond tile in play. Players will build the bamboo garden outward from this central pond.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each turn follows this sequence:

1. **Determine Weather** — Roll the weather die and apply its effect (see Weather Effects below).
2. **Perform 2 Actions** — Choose 2 different actions from the 5 available actions. You cannot take the same action twice in one turn (unless modified by weather).
3. **Check Objectives** — At any time during or after your actions, you may reveal completed objective cards and score them immediately.

#### Weather Effects
- **Sun** — Take a 3rd action of your choice (can duplicate).
- **Rain** — Place 1 bamboo section on an irrigated plot of your choice.
- **Wind** — You may take the same action twice this turn.
- **Storm** — Move the panda to any plot; it eats 1 bamboo section there.
- **Clouds** — Take 1 improvement chip from the supply and place on your board.
- **?** (Question Mark) — Choose any weather effect above.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### The 5 Actions

**1. Draw 3 Plot Tiles**
- Draw 3 plot tiles (1 of each color or from the same stack).
- Choose 1 to place adjacent to an existing tile.
- Return the other 2 to the bottom of their stacks.
- Tiles must connect edge-to-edge (not corner-to-corner).

**2. Take an Irrigation Channel**
- Take 1 irrigation channel from the supply and place it on your individual board.
- You may immediately place it on the garden between two plots.
- A plot becomes irrigated when connected to the pond via irrigation channels or when adjacent to the pond.

**3. Move the Gardener**
- Move the gardener in a straight line to any plot.
- Grow 1 bamboo section on the plot where the gardener stops AND all adjacent plots of the same color (if irrigated).
- Bamboo stacks are limited to 4 sections high.

**4. Move the Panda**
- Move the panda in a straight line to any plot.
- The panda eats 1 bamboo section from that plot.
- You take the eaten bamboo and place it on your individual board.

**5. Draw an Objective Card**
- Draw 1 objective card from any of the 3 decks (plot, panda, or gardener).
- Maximum hand limit: 5 objective cards total.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Irrigation Rules
- Plots are irrigated if: (1) adjacent to the pond, OR (2) connected to the pond via a chain of irrigation channels.
- Only irrigated plots can grow bamboo.
- The pond tile is always considered irrigated.

#### Improvements
You can place improvements on plots when you place new plot tiles. Each improvement type has unique effects:
- **Watershed** — Plot is permanently irrigated (doesn't need irrigation channels).
- **Fertilizer** — When bamboo grows here, it grows 2 sections instead of 1.
- **Enclosure** — The panda cannot eat bamboo from this plot.

#### Objective Types
- **Plot Objectives** — Complete specific patterns/configurations of colored plots.
- **Panda Objectives** — Collect specific combinations of eaten bamboo.
- **Gardener Objectives** — Grow bamboo to specific heights and colors.

#### Movement Rules
- Both the gardener and panda move in straight lines only (not diagonally).
- They can move any distance in that line.
- Movement always triggers their effect on the destination plot.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Endgame Trigger
The game ends immediately when a player completes their final objective card to reach the threshold:
- **2 Players** — 9 completed objectives
- **3 Players** — 8 completed objectives
- **4 Players** — 7 completed objectives

The player who triggers the end receives the Emperor card worth 2 bonus points. All other players take one final turn.

#### Scoring
- Add up the point values on all your completed objective cards.
- Add 2 points if you have the Emperor card.
- Highest total wins.

#### Tiebreaker
If tied, the player with the most points from panda objectives wins. If still tied, players share the victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game Focus
- **Get Irrigation Early** — Secure irrigation channels in your first few turns so you can grow bamboo consistently.
- **Diversify Objectives** — Hold a mix of objective types (plot, panda, gardener) to capitalize on different board states.
- **Build Color Clusters** — Group same-colored plots together to maximize gardener growth efficiency.
- **Weather Die Awareness** — Plan actions that benefit from likely weather effects (Sun for 3rd action, Wind for doubling valuable actions).

#### Objective Hand Management
- Don't fill your hand to 5 objectives too quickly.
- Focus on objectives that overlap in requirements.
- Complete easier objectives first to maintain tempo toward the endgame trigger.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Forgetting Irrigation** — Trying to grow bamboo on non-irrigated plots (bamboo only grows where plots are irrigated).
- **Same Action Twice** — Taking the same action twice without Wind weather (only Wind allows duplicate actions).
- **Gardener Growth Spread** — Forgetting that the gardener grows bamboo on ALL adjacent same-color plots, not just the destination.
- **Movement Restrictions** — Moving panda or gardener diagonally (they can only move in straight lines).
- **Objective Hand Limit** — Exceeding 5 objective cards in hand.
- **Missing Final Turns** — Forgetting that all other players get one final turn after someone triggers the endgame.
- **Improvement Timing** — Trying to place improvements after a plot is already placed (improvements must be placed when the plot tile is placed).

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### When to Draw Objectives vs Execute
- **Draw early** when your hand has few objectives or you've just completed several.
- **Execute actions** when you have 3+ objectives that need board development.
- Balance your hand around 3-4 objectives in mid-game.

#### Gardener vs Panda Movement
- **Gardener** — Better for building long-term value and completing gardener objectives.
- **Panda** — Better for immediate panda objectives and disrupting opponents' tall bamboo.
- Watch what opponents need and deny strategically.

#### Racing to Endgame
- **Trigger early** if you're ahead on points and opponents have few completed objectives.
- **Slow the game** by focusing on plot placement and irrigation if you need more time.
- Track opponents' completed objective counts (visible face-up cards).

#### Improvement Placement
- **Watershed** — Use on isolated plots or to save irrigation channel actions.
- **Fertilizer** — Place on colors you need to grow quickly for gardener objectives.
- **Enclosure** — Use defensively on plots you need to keep tall for objectives.

metadata.notes: OCR stub - no readable rulebook text available. All content added from Claude knowledge (Feb 2026). Content covers: components, setup, turn structure, weather die effects, 5 actions, irrigation rules, improvements, objectives, endgame triggers (2P=9/3P=8/4P=7 objectives), Emperor card bonus, scoring, and strategy. Core mechanics: roll weather die, take 2 different actions (plot placement/irrigation/move gardener/move panda/draw objective), complete objectives for points. Designer: Antoine Bauza. Recommend verifying specific component counts and objective card point values against physical rulebook.
